mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when UNRATE spikes, or the signal fails to predict direction in >70% of threshold-crossing events.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand, supporting corporate revenues and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and reduced economic activity, triggering short-term risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in >55% of observations during tightening periods, or the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS is flat.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation remains persistently above 3.0%, markets price in prolonged restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive edge over a simple moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a resilient labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP reflects broad-based economic growth that translates into higher corporate earnings, generating positive forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 60-day SPX returns, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals an imminent supply shock or recessionary pressure, prompting portfolio de-risking and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >70% of months following a >0.5% INDPRO drop, or the threshold signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
